U.S. stock indices experienced their strongest weekly gains of 2026, with the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq all closing higher as investors reacted to geopolitical developments in the Strait of Hormuz. Despite this positive momentum, the Dow faced downward pressure due to comments from former President Trump that tempered optimism regarding a potential resolution to the ongoing tensions with Iran.

The surge in oil prices, which recorded the largest one-day gain since 2020, is a key factor influencing market sentiment. Rising crude prices can impact inflation expectations and consumer spending, while also affecting sectors such as energy and transportation. Investors are closely monitoring these developments, as they could lead to increased volatility in the markets.

As oil prices remain a focal point, market professionals should consider the implications for sector rotation and portfolio adjustments, particularly in energy stocks, which may benefit from sustained higher prices amid geopolitical uncertainty.

Source: [news.google.com](https://news.google.?oc=5)
